reviews.review_title: Neat XR app with an active developer
reviews.review_description:
I first played this game ~15 days ago, and had issues during startup (hand tracking, freezing) and multiple annoyances during gameplay. The hand tracking issue at the start was so bad that at some point I though about refunding, but after restarting once I never had that issue again. Still, after playing for some time I didn't like it enough. I didn't want to leave a completely negative review so instead I decided to contact the developer, I detailed my annoyances, and included some suggestions about potential improvements, and finally I uninstalled the game.

A week later, the developer invited me to the beta testing channel, saying they kept working on it, fixed most of the issues I listed, and were actively working on the remaining. I decided to give it another go, and two days ago I installed it again, started it up, and was positively surprised that I had no issues during startup or when playing! Not only that, but the UI was improved, hand tracking was working fine, and even controller support was added! And the interactions were improved too! I no longer need to twist my wrist if I want to rotate a flower!

I'm giving it 4 stars because it's a neat idea, and it executed mostly well, but it still needs to be a bit more polished. The developer told me that they're also working on some new features, so this time I'm keeping the application installed (it's pretty small anyway), and I'm looking forward to updating my review in the future!

The trial had a hand tracking bug when I tried it but I bought it anyway.  This is fixed in the full version.  
Performance isn't great though on a Quest 2.  I can see my hands reprojecting which is kind of annoying.   
I always like unique VR experiences and this one is pretty good.  Relaxing music and playing with flowers is more fun than expected.  Needs more flowers though.  Not paid DLC it just needs more content.  And it would be nice if you could move the arch because it's very close to your face when playing lying down mode.

developer_response.body: Thank your for purchasing the app and giving us a honest feedback! New updates coming very soon, including more flowers 🌷

reviews.review_description:
This app is great for creating unique floral arrangements in a very relaxed atmosphere, although I would prefer different music or none at all. 
The flowers & vases are beautiful and easy to arrange. I was disappointed that I was unable to move my completed vase with flowers onto my kitchen table in AR mode; I wish my entire arrangement stayed together & moved as one unit.
I thought it might be a plus if there was a speaker icon next to each item in the book that would correctly pronounce the name (in a language a user could pick in settings).

The Quiz game is fun & an excellent way to strengthen & test the user’s knowledge. I liked the idea of the Challenge game, however, I was unable to reach the target while sitting. Perhaps the Challenge could also include copying an arrangement previously made rather than just collecting items. (Looking at finished arrangements also helps creativity.)

I would love to see other educational apps such as this for gardening. Perhaps some “how to” instructional apps that let you apply hands-on practice would be of interest & beneficial to learn a trade or job.

I received a free code for this app in a drawing.

My first impression is that the MR mode is nice and the graphic detail is good. The controls were not super intuitive, but I did figure out how to arrange some flowers in the vase. 

I'm not sure how often I'll go back to this app, but I can see how it would be relaxing for someone with more interest in the flowers. I could definitely see a florist using this to mock up arrangements.

Not my usual type of game or experience, but always willing to experiment. Quite relaxing even though I don’t have an ‘artsy’ eye.  Nice touch having MR as I was able to place my not so good efforts around my rooms, it would be great to know what the flowers are I am picking to use, so a flower name on the book page would be amazing.  It would be great to be able to play relaxing music my own such as access to ‘youtube/spotify’.  Also it would be beneficial to have a friend/co-op mode where people who are good at this sort of thing can meet up in a room chat and give advice and perhaps you could take skills learnt and use irl. And the additions of controllers rather than just hand tracking may help. I hope the devs will update this in due course to include my suggestions..
Loved it and looking forward to new updates and new flowers!

reviews.review_title: App with therapeutic effect & active dev
reviews.review_description:
Nice App with therapeutic effect when you wanna relax.
Lots of potential 
Active dev and is open to feedback

Hope there would be higher quality textures available for Quest 3

reviews.review_title: Perfect MR and beautiful VR flowers
reviews.review_description:
Each flower is a piece of art in how well and precisely they've been rendered. You can turn them around and look at them from every angle and marvel at how beautiful they are.
I was impressed by how real my compositions looked and wished I could have taken snapshots of them to keep them, hopefully this will come in a future release! It would be nice to also have a choice of musics and be able to change or stop the sky everfalling petals...
I tried using this while sitting on my bed, at first, but that didn't work so well with the pillar holding the vase being a bit too low. A minor annoyance as I just decided not to have it in the end. I'm looking forward to future releases.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for such a detalied and costructive feedback! 
We continue working to improve the experience and add more features.
At the moment the user can take snapshots or record video from the built-in functions of the Meta Quest headsets if you want to have a picture of your creations or record the entire creation process. We will keep in mind to add these commands inside the app too

reviews.review_description: I like this app! It's very relaxing to try all new flower types and compositions. It works good in MR and it's satisfying!
I love this to play on chilled evenings and to show it to friends and family.

Very intressthing idea. And it's very calming. The flowers look good and the handtracking is decent. It's still not a finished product. But what works does work well sure there is room for improvement.

But I can see myself playing this for hours.

I got the key from FB GA.
So beautiful folwers and comfortable BGM and MR play is so nice to play happily relaxed.
Someday more flowers and multiplay mode we hope.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! New flowers coming very soon!

reviews.review_description:
The app works great on the Quest Pro, and the augmented reality is impressive.

I became immersed in carefully placing the flowers to make some arrangements, and found myself becoming quite obsessed with placing them, perhaps revealing an unknown horticulturist I was previously unaware of!

The developers have spent a lot of time on this application and it certainly is refreshing to see something different on the Quest Pro

I didn’t know I like being a florist until now. Really relaxing journey, even the music hits just perfectly ☺️

My suggestion/feedback:

As I lost myself in the process of creating flower arrangements with some I didn't even know existed, I found myself wishing for more options and variations. 

Maybe you could add different arch designs and more decorations so it feels like you can personalise it further.


🌺🌷🍊🌸

The idea of ​​this app is great. You can surround yourself with flowers in your own space and relax. However, there is still a lot of potential for improvement.

Inventory in the room:

An arch with branches will appear, which can be decorated with flowers. There is also a pedestal with a flower pot to fill.

This inventory cannot be moved or removed and it prevents me from freely creating a flower paradise in my room.

You can change the design of the only flower pot but not move it. I think it would be nice if I could choose pots and distribute them around my room. Otherwise I can only place the flowers on the arch, in the air or on a piece of furniture without a pot.

Controllers:

It would be nice if you could also use the controllers instead of hand tracking.

Graphic:

The branches on the arch flicker. Likewise the flowers, if they are not directly in front of the face. I find this disturbing and prevents me from relaxing.

Save:

If you leave the app and re-enter, every design is gone and not saved.

Price:

The price for this simple app is too expensive based on what other apps in this price range offer.

Potential:

A nice idea that should be expanded further!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback and suggestions! The new version will be released soon, which includes some of your suggestions already such as support for controllers (and Quest2), ability to move the Flower Book around :)

reviews.review_description:
Needs some work, hand tracking seems touch n go.  I pick up and flower and place it in the vase then as I go to grab another flower, the previous flower I just placed returns to my hand. Becomes frustrating.  

Once controller support is added I may try the game again.  Has potential  !
A relaxing flowering experience. Looking forward to a way of manipulating multiple flowers simultaneously.
